pages.raw_text:
There will be no relief for the
shortage of coal production in this
state as far as the immense hold
ings of the Northern Pacific is con-
concerned unless that company
elects to dispose of some of its
coal lands to others.


The provisions of the interstate
commerce act, which became effec-
tive January / of this year, for
bids railroad company from en-
gaging in the mining of coal to be
shipped over its own lines except
ing for its own consumption. The
Northern Pacific has announced that
it is going out of the coal business
as far as the public is concerned,
at the same time declaring that its
present production Of coal is not
more than Is required for its own
use.


Three members Of the interstate
commerce commission, who begin
sittings here on January IA, may
take up the coal situation as sub
ject of investigation.


Not being permitted to sell coal
from its own mines the Northern
Pacific has been unwilling to furn-
ish cars for fuel shipment for pri-
vate mining companies.


This is proved by the railroad
